lint.observation:
[ticket-parity] OK: no ticket.yaml present; skipping parity check
[knowledgebase-lint] OK

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add the exact NON-CANONICAL / REFERENCE ONLY banner markdown template to CONVENTIONS.md §6 so future workers have a canonical format to follow rather than inventing their own banner style.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature docs-consolidation-legacy-redirects established a consistent banner pattern (blockquote with ⚠️ icon, bold label, relative replacement links) across 12 legacy docs, but the exact template is not codified in CONVENTIONS.md. CONVENTIONS.md §6.2 describes the policy (redirect, archive, mark non-canonical) but does not show the markdown template. This could lead to drift if future workers invent their own banner style.

rejectedObservations.observation: ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md and migration-guide.md remain without top-level NON-CANONICAL banners despite mentioning live endpoints/behavior.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md claims to be authoritative and has inline [NON-CANONICAL] annotations on specific sections. Whether these need top-level banners depends on scoping decisions that are the orchestrator's to make, not the scrutiny validator's.

nonBlockingIssues.description: The 'Accounting and Reporting' section in docs/deprecated/INDEX.md references 'accounting-period-close flow' but entries like 'Audit digest CSV export' arguably relate more to the reporting-export flow. The reporting-export flow link is already present in the next section. Consider updating to reference both flows or reorganizing.
The exact NON-CANONICAL/REFERENCE ONLY banner markdown template is not codified in docs/CONVENTIONS.md. CONVENTIONS.md §6.2 describes the policy but not the template format.
